behaviors.description: Embedding vector has correct dimensionality for vector DB schema
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: A mocked embedding service can return any dimension without detecting the mismatch. REAL-INFRA: The real embedding model (local or API) must be called to verify output shape matches the DB schema (e.g., 1536 dimensions). A unit test with a mock would pass even if the production embedding service changed from 1536 to 768 dimensions, causing DB insertion failures. The failure—dimensional mismatch—only surfaces when the real embedding service is invoked.
behaviors.test_description: Call the embedding service with test queries and verify each returned vector has dimensionality exactly matching the vector database schema (e.g., 1536 for OpenAI text-embedding-3-small); repeat across several queries to ensure consistency.

behaviors.description: Similarity search returns results ordered by descending cosine similarity score
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: A mocked vector DB can return results in any order without catching ordering bugs. REAL-INFRA: A real (or test) vector database must be queried to verify actual ordering behavior. The failure—results returned in wrong order—is invisible to unit tests with mocked DB responses. Integration-level testing with a populated test vector database (deterministic, controlled) is sufficient since cosine similarity ranking is deterministic.
behaviors.test_description: Perform similarity search on a test vector database populated with documents of known similarity distance to the query; verify returned results are in strictly descending order by similarity score, and that the top-1 result has the highest score.

behaviors.description: LLM re-ranking reorders results when query has nuanced intent
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Mocking the LLM to return a pre-determined reordering fails to verify the real LLM understands nuance. LLM-DEP: Correctness depends entirely on real LLM behavior—whether it actually re-ranks for nuanced queries (e.g., 'affordable luxury'). Different LLMs or model versions may re-rank differently. MULTI-STEP: Single re-ranking call, but outcome is non-deterministic. A unit test with a stubbed LLM always returns the same order, hiding whether the real LLM applies contextual reasoning. Mocking removes all signal about whether the actual model achieves the intended reordering.
behaviors.test_description: Run the LLM re-ranker on similarity-ranked results for queries with nuanced or ambiguous intent (e.g., 'cheap but high-quality', 'budget luxury'); call the real LLM and verify that at least some result positions differ from the input order; run multiple times to confirm the LLM consistently applies contextual reasoning and to capture variance in re-ranking behavior.

behaviors.description: Query with no close matches returns empty set, not low-relevance noise
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: A mock vector DB returning an empty list would pass the test regardless of whether the code actually filters by similarity threshold. REAL-INFRA: A test vector database with actual data and similarity scores is needed to verify the filtering logic works on real scores. The failure—low-score results are not filtered and returned to the user—only appears when querying a real DB that returns borderline-similarity matches. Unit test with a mock DB cannot detect if the threshold is missing or misconfigured.
behaviors.test_description: Populate a test vector database with products from a specific domain (e.g., electronics), then query with a distinctly unrelated phrase (e.g., 'philosophy of mind' or 'ancient poetry'); verify the API returns an empty result set, confirming low-similarity results are filtered by threshold rather than returned; verify the threshold is enforced consistently across multiple unrelated queries.

behaviors.description: End-to-end latency (embed → search → re-rank → return) stays below 2 seconds median
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Mocking embedding model, vector DB, and LLM all return instantly, hiding real latency. REAL-INFRA: Real external API calls (embedding, vector DB, LLM) are required to measure actual latency. LLM-DEP: LLM latency varies non-deterministically across runs depending on request complexity, model state, and server load—this variance is critical to validate SLA compliance. The failure—real latency exceeds 2 seconds in production—is completely invisible when all services are mocked. A System-level test with real infrastructure is necessary, but Agentic captures the fact that LLM latency variance is the dominant factor in whether the SLA is met.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the full pipeline (query → embedding → vector search → LLM re-ranking → response) with real external services (embedding API, vector database, and LLM re-ranker); measure wall-clock time across 50+ runs using realistic product search queries; verify median latency stays below 2 seconds and capture percentile distribution (p50, p95, p99); the test must use the real LLM (not a mock) because LLM latency varies with request complexity and semantic depth, and this variance significantly impacts SLA compliance.
